cards.back: A binary tree is a tree data structure in which each node has at most two children (i.e., left child and right child)

cards.front: What is the purpose of a Root Node in a Binary Tree?
cards.back: The root node is the topmost node in the binary tree and serves as the starting point for traversing or searching the tree

cards.front: What is a Recursive Traversal of a Binary Tree?
cards.back: A recursive traversal is a method of traversing a binary tree by visiting each node and then recursively visiting its child nodes

cards.front: What is the difference between an Inorder Traversal and a Preorder Traversal of a Binary Tree?
cards.back: An inorder traversal visits the left subtree, the root node, and then the right subtree, while a preorder traversal visits the root node, then the left subtree, and then the right subtree

cards.front: What is a Depth-First Search (DFS) traversal?
cards.back: A DFS traversal is a method of traversing a graph or tree by traversing as far as possible along each branch before backtracking

cards.front: What is the purpose of a Postorder Traversal of a Binary Tree?
cards.back: A postorder traversal visits the left subtree, the right subtree, and then the root node, which is useful for deleting a node and its children

cards.front: What is the difference between a Binary Search Tree (BST) and a Regular Binary Tree?
cards.back: A BST has the property that all the values in the left subtree are less than the root node, and all the values in the right subtree are greater than the root node

cards.front: What is a Min-Heap in the context of Binary Search Trees?
cards.back: A min-heap is a complete binary tree where the parent node is less than or equal to its child nodes, and the root node is the minimum value in the tree

cards.front: What is a Max-Heap in the context of Binary Search Trees?
cards.back: A max-heap is a complete binary tree where the parent node is greater than or equal to its child nodes, and the root node is the maximum value in the tree

cards.front: What is the purpose of a Node in a Binary Tree?
cards.back: A node in a binary tree is an individual element that contains a value and zero or more child nodes
